Review: Easily the best professor I have taken at DePaul. Actually cares about her students and formats the lectures in a digestible way. Also always available outside of class and during office hours. Very happy with this class, would 100 percent take again!

Department: Physics

Difficulty: 3.0
For Credit: Yes

Would Take Again: Yes
Grade: Not sure yet
Textbook: Yes
Review: Landahl is a relaxed but incredibly knowledgeable teacher. The grade consists of your in-class work, quizzes that are every other week, midterm project (which has instructions to go with it) and a final paper. His TA Gabe is awesome as hell
Tags: Caring, Respected

nstructions to go with it) and a final paper. His TA Gabe is awesome as hell

Date: Aug 22nd, 2022
Quality: 5.0

For Credit: Yes
Attendance: Mandatory
Would Take Again: Yes
Grade: B+
Textbook: Yes
Review: The most accessible and available professor, very attentive to his students and caring about your progress as a future physicist. I took both Electronics and Experimental Physics with him. Both were fulfilling, necessary, and taught by someone who clearly understands his field and how his teachings apply into the workforce and professional fields.

k a different class for your lab requirement. Dr. Landhal was a fine teacher and willing to help. If you're interested in the material you could learn a lot. and we kind of just had to figure things out on our own, so it was frustrating. The tests weren't that bad, he's an easy grader and he gives extra credit, so that's the upside.

For Credit: Yes
Textbook: Yes
Review: He doesn't lecture at all but that's not how you learn physics. It's a lab based class where you observe the material you're learning, then analyze your data to solve problems. Even without lecturing you learn so much, you just have to be willing to put in the effort. Lots of extra credit and exams aren't bad at all.

The workload is huge and you have to teach yourself. He should NOT be paid to teach since he does not do it. Professor reviews

Review: Gen physics is already messed up at DePaul (you don't get taught), but he took it to the next level! He never lectured (he did twice actually but about easy math stuff). It also seemed like he was never

never lectured (he did twice actually but about easy math stuff). It also seemed like he was never willing to help. That is, when he was even around (he studies at Argon lab). Arrogant, disrespectful, unclear, & unprepared. Absolutely awful

as extremely prepared to teach the material. And sure the homework was hard, but he always made sure we were prepared for tests.

rying to teach us while coding. She uploads her code which usually works if I try it on my own. She doesn't go over the homework inclass
